problem: The #ticket feature is primarily a web UI (helix-global-client) and orchestrator (helix-global-server) concern. The helix-cli is a lightweight command-line tool with no interactive prompt/textarea UI where hashtag detection would occur. The CLI may be context-only for this ticket.

files.why: CLI entry point. Simple process.argv command router. No interactive prompt UI exists.
Comment subcommand router. Uses --ticket flag or HELIX_TICKET_ID env var for ticket context. This is the closest the CLI gets to ticket referencing.

facts: The CLI is a non-interactive command-line tool with simple process.argv parsing. There is no textarea, prompt UI, or interactive input where # detection would occur.
The CLI has no autocomplete or suggestion mechanism.
The #ticket feature as described (autocomplete dropdown, filtering, inline references in a textarea) is inherently a web UI feature that belongs in helix-global-client.
The CLI communicates with the server via REST API (src/lib/http.ts) but only uses /api/inspect/* and /api/tickets/{id}/comments endpoints.
No changes to helix-cli are expected for this ticket.
unknowns: Could a future CLI use case benefit from a 'hlx ticket context <ticketId>' command to dump referenced ticket context? (Out of scope for this ticket)
